Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; And Declaration Of Thomas Marc Litton In Support Of Motion To Compel Responses By Defendant Hansen To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Set One
Off Calendar Aug-27-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Thomas Litton, Esq. (415-421-4774; marc@littonlaw.com) of Law Offices of Thomas Marc Litton for Plaintiff.
David Dalby, Esq. (415-362-6000; ddalby@hinshawlaw.com) of Hinshaw & Colbertson LLP for Defendants.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF LANIE MENARD's Motion To Compel Responses By Defendant Hansen To Plaintiff's Request For Production Of Documents, Set One:

Plaintiff's Motions to Compel Responses to Form Interrogatories, Set One, and Requests for Production of Documents, Set One are GRANTED, and Plaintiff's Motion to Have Requests for Admission, Set One, Deemed Admitted is GRANTED IN PART and DENIED IN PART. Defendant must respond without objection to the three discovery requests at issue by September 9, 2021. Plaintiff's requests for monetary sanctions as against Defendant Hansen is GRANTED in the amount of $1,335.00 in total for all three motions, and DENIED to the extent that Plaintiff seeks further sanctions. re: sanctions: without prejudice as to any showing Defendant Hansen did not receive notice; burden on Hansen to file for relief from sanctions.

Counsel for plaintiff to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312 via email to mpritt@bsfllp.com

Judge Pro Tem: Maxwell Pritt, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
